title: PDR-020 slice2 (lead-mgwb): carried 172 vs 175 contradict on dummyco render — bc-base image literal
description: Carried scenario 172 (03f1256aefc7fad4) has an Examples table with BOTH shopsystem and dummyco, and its step requires the rendered bin/shop-shell to contain the FIXED literal substring 'shopsystem-bc-base' for EVERY example row (including dummyco). Likely intended fix: make 172's bc-base image reference slug-parametric (<slug>-bc-base) so the dummyco row renders 'dummyco-bc-base', OR drop dummyco from 172's Examples. Needs a lead-authored re-pin. Emitting clarify.

title: request_bugfix lead-3rda: bc-emit work-done _CARVE_OUTS → deliverable-scope clean-tree precondition; retire 176, add 212 (scenario cba037e97c6a8325)
notes:
Lead work_id: lead-3rda
mechanism_observation: bc-emit work-done Check 3 STALE scan is global across features/: one drifted tag blocks every wrapper sign-off — While dogfooding the canonical reviewer sign-off path (`bc-emit work-done`, scenario 210) for lead-3rda, the wrapper passed its now-deliverable-scoped clean-tree Check 1 on a non-deliverable-only dirty tree (the lead-3rda fix worked) but then REFUSED on Check 3 (`check_scenario_hashes`) with classification STALE for `bc_implementer_sections.feature` tag `1758fe166d638cda` (recompute `3314ed2e84ad54c5`). That tag is PRE-EXISTING (present at parent 6f22fe5, untouched by lead-3rda) and unrelated to the dispatched scope. The load-bearing issue: `check_scenario_hashes` recomputes and STALE-checks EVERY block in EVERY `features/*.feature`, not just the payload's reported hashes or the work_id's touched files. Consequence: any single drifted tag anywhere in features/ makes the wrapper refuse for ALL work_ids, so the canonical wrapper sign-off (scenario 210) is non-functional shop-wide until that unrelated tag is repaired, forcing every reviewer onto the bare `shop-msg respond` forced-recovery path and defeating the wrapper's purpose. The next BC's reviewer will hit the same global refusal on any dispatch and may wrongly attribute it to their own work. Worth considering: scope the STALE refusal to payload-reported hashes (or to features/ files the work_id touched), and surface unrelated whole-tree drift as a non-blocking warning plus a tracked repair task, rather than a hard refusal that blocks unrelated sign-offs.
close_reason: lead-3rda work_done complete (reviewer signed off 212; retired 176)

title: Reconcile the CLI-layer "bc-emit work-done" wrapper clean-working-tree precondition (ADR-036 D1/D2, src/shop_templates/bc_emit.py _CARVE_OUTS) to the work-done-gate skill Check 1 DELIVERABLE-SCOPE mod

description: Grow converged_ops_scaffolding.feature scenario b764caa1dea99fcb to enumerate seven shop-owned ops files (add bin/ops-coordinates), update backing step-def, recompute block-only @scenario_hash.
notes: clarify: Scenario d5d65b9cfedc24c1 (211) lists OPS_BC_BEADS_REPO_FMT among the keys that sourcing bin/ops-coordinates must DEFINE, and requires every OPS_* assignment be env-overridable ${OVERRIDE:-default} form, but it never pins OPS_BC_BEADS_REPO_FMT's RESOLVED value. Those two requirements interact destructively: the override-form wrapping OPS_BC_BEADS_REPO_FMT="${OPS_BC_BEADS_REPO_FMT:-<slug>-{bc}-beads}" makes bash close the parameter expansion on the FIRST } (the one after {bc), so 'source bin/ops-coordinates' yields <slug>-{bc-beads} (verified for both example slugs) -- the literal {bc} placeholder the key's own comment says 'a consumer fills with a bounded-context name' is destroyed, and ${FMT/\{bc\}/<bc>} substitution no longer matches. The render renders the right TEMPLATE token ({{OPS_SLUG}}-{bc}-beads) but the env-overridable ${:-} wrapper this scenario mandates corrupts it at source time. Every other pinned behavior is faithful (211 both rows, grown converged_ops 0a7e4c29fc7db52b, carried-exactly-once invariants, runtime coherence of all shop-shell keys). Should 211 add a Then step pinning that after sourcing OPS_BC_BEADS_REPO_FMT resolves to <slug>-{bc}-beads with the literal {bc} placeholder INTACT (forcing the render to escape the brace, e.g. {bc\} via printf-safe form, or to render FMT OUTSIDE the ${:-} default) -- or is 'defined non-empty' the intended Phase-1 contract with placeholder integrity deferred to the future consumer-wiring work_id? FMT currently has no consumer, but the corrupt default is baked into every bootstrapped shop now.
close_reason: ow4d GROW sub-issue; converged_ops grown to 0a7e4c29fc7db52b (landed, lockstep held by lead-0act)

description: Remove scenario blocks 725562869d9df919 (172), 166b86d779ecd0e7 (175), a3b723341d9f2872 (134) from features/converged_ops_scaffolding.feature and realign the image-literal/data-literal assertions in tests/test_ops_converged_shop_shell.py and tests/test_ops_generification.py. Keep 198 (84a32b05666d9e82), 174/137 file-set, 138 pgdata green.
notes: mechanism_observation: bc-emit work-done _CARVE_OUTS narrower than work-done-gate deliverable-scope; forces routine --force escapes — The executable `bc-emit work-done` wrapper refuses the emit when the tree carries `.claude/canonical/bc-primer.md` or `.claude/settings.json` dirty, because its `_CARVE_OUTS` set only discounts `.specstory`, `.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock`, and `.beads/issues.jsonl`. But the AUTHORITATIVE work-done-gate Check 1 is deliverable-scoped: it blocks ONLY on dirty paths under `features/`/`src/`/`tests/`, and its prose EXPLICITLY enumerates `.claude/settings.json` and `.claude/canonical/bc-primer.md` as non-deliverable harness paths that do NOT block. So the wrapper refuses on exactly the paths the prose says are clean. The harness routinely edits both files during a session, so this false-refusal fires on essentially every scenario-carrying sign-off, pushing the reviewer onto the bare `shop-msg respond ... --force` escape valve as the ROUTINE path. Load-bearing risk: habituating reviewers to --force past the wrapper means they also lose the wrapper's REAL protections (deliverable-dirty tree, unreachable work_id, retired-hash-still-present) on the forced path; a genuinely-dirty deliverable tree could be force-emitted without the gate running. Here those wrapper checks (reachability + retire-hash for 172/175/134) DID pass, so nothing was lost this time, but the routine reliance on --force is the erosion.

title: Tighten existing-but-mis-stated bc-reviewer template prose so the routine sign-off work_done emit is directed through the "bc-emit work-done" WRAPPER (which runs the gate/precondition checks incl. sce
TDD: provision approve-claude writes NON-EMPTY CLAUDE_OAUTH (capture+writeback), aborts on empty (scenario 1c054dfdc468860a)
notes: mechanism_observation: work-done-gate Check 4 RED-sub-issue criterion is ambiguous; a combined 'TDD:' sub-issue degrades its signal — Check 4 of the work-done-gate requires '>=1 sub-issue ... explicit failing-test (RED) sub-issue (title contains "write the failing test for" or similar RED nomenclature)'. For lead-dui6 the only sub-issue (tmpl-i7e, closed) is titled 'TDD: provision approve-claude writes NON-EMPTY CLAUDE_OAUTH (capture+writeback), aborts on empty' — a single combined TDD-cycle task, NOT a failing-test/RED-named sub-issue. This BC's OWN established convention files explicit RED sub-issues (e.g. tmpl-2wu.1/.3 'write the failing test for ...', tmpl-3yu 'write the failing test (RED) for ...', tmpl-caa, tmpl-d7n.1). I signed off because the load-bearing TDD evidence — Check 5's GENUINE red (test(red) bcb3313 fails at the red commit, precedes feat(green) b1edbcd) — unambiguously confirms test-first discipline, so bouncing faithful, discriminating, additive work on a sub-issue-title technicality would be form-over-substance. But the gate's 'or similar RED nomenclature' fuzz makes Check 4 non-discriminating: it cannot tell a combined 'TDD:' sub-issue apart from a real RED sub-issue, so it silently degrades to near-zero signal whenever the implementer collapses RED/GREEN into one 'TDD:' task. This recurs on every TDD dispatch. Either the gate prose should sharpen the criterion (require a sub-issue whose title names RED / failing-test) and the implementer-side planning must file it, or the gate should explicitly accept a single 'TDD:'-labelled sub-issue and lean on Check 5 for the real test-first proof — but the current ambiguity lets the two roles drift apart.

title: TDD: bring-up-bc SKILL.md scopes BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME to bind-mounted-home, drops universal always-set (scenario 7df4006ce0d43d8b)
notes: mechanism_observation: shop-msg respond work_done accepts --scenario-hash not pinned in features/ (no emit-time Check-3 subset enforcement) — While signing off lead-jonx I emitted work_done --status complete with a typo'd second hash 'cc52003444ee7' (the real pinned hash for scenario 161 is 'cc52003444ea63f7'). shop-msg accepted and persisted the bad hash into the lead-inbox outbox without complaint; I caught it only by post-emit re-read and re-emitted with --force. The work-done-gate skill's Check 3 (recompute hashes + git grep features/ for each hash + enforce subset) is documented as the reviewer's MANUAL responsibility, but the emit CLI itself performs no cross-check of --scenario-hash against the committed features/ tree, even though it runs in-repo with features/ available. This is the same class of emit-fidelity defect the gate exists to prevent (cf. the lead-cw7 placeholder-summary case), and a hash-not-in-features/ is even more mechanically checkable than a placeholder string: a single reviewer transcription slip writes an ADR-010-violating hash to the lead's inbox, where it can mis-attribute a pass to a scenario that was never pinned (or, with a prefix-style typo, to the wrong scenario). It is load-bearing because every reviewer work_done emit across every BC routes through this CLI and currently trusts the caller's hashes verbatim.
close_reason: RED->GREEN complete: bring-up-bc SKILL.md scoped BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME to bind-mounted-home case, dropped universal always-set; scenario 7df4006ce0d43d8b passes, scenario 161 preserved

title: request_bugfix lead-b6f2: bin/shop-shell seeds non-empty placeholder GH_TOKEN into launched leaf-BC session (scenario 0789db8bb7f3bc73)
notes:
Lead work_id: lead-b6f2
mechanism_observation: bc-emit work-done _CARVE_OUTS narrower than work-done-gate Check 1; false-refuses emits on dirty harness/config paths — The executable `bc-emit work-done` clean-tree precondition discounts only the ambient carve-outs .specstory, .claude/scheduled_tasks.lock, .beads/issues.jsonl. But the work-done-gate skill's Check 1 is DELIVERABLE-SCOPED: it blocks only on dirty paths under features/ src/ tests/, and explicitly names .claude/canonical/bc-primer.md and .claude/settings.json as NON-deliverable harness/config paths that DO NOT block. The wrapper and its own specified gate therefore disagree. On this review the BC tree carried in-flight harness edits to .claude/canonical/bc-primer.md (a bc-router session-start protocol rewrite) and .claude/settings.json (SessionStart hooks reorder) — neither part of the lead-b6f2 deliverable, neither safe to commit under this work_id nor to revert (another role's in-flight work). The wrapper refused the sign-off even though all five gate checks pass and the deliverable tree (features/ src/ tests/) is clean. This is load-bearing: the harness rewrites bc-primer.md / settings.json at session-start routinely, so the wrapper will false-refuse a large fraction of reviewer work_done emits, forcing reviewers onto the bare shop-msg respond escape valve and thereby BYPASSING the wrapper's own reachability + scenario-hash re-validation — the opposite of the wrapper's intent.
close_reason: lead-b6f2 work_done complete (reviewer signed off scenario 0789db8bb7f3bc73)

title: FIX TARGET: canonical thin bin/shop-shell template (shipped by shopsystem-templates as package data, re-poured into the lead shop at templates v0.21.0 / commit 37d0a7f). Two defects in the INNER `bc-c
notes:
Lead work_id: lead-ss6k
clarify: GAP (ADR-010 blocker, NOT a code-fix gap): lead-ss6k mandates SUPERSEDING lead-held scenario 172 (@scenario_hash:da8ea23429b8bdc9, pinned BC-locally in features/converged_ops_scaffolding.feature) — its Then clause hard-pins the leaf image by literal substring "shopsystem-bc-base", which DEFECT 2's fix makes WRONG (leaf must be shopsystem-bc-lead). But the dispatch carried scenarios: [] — NO re-authored 172 Gherkin and NO replacement @scenario_hash. Per ADR-010 (work_done scenario_hashes must be a SUBSET of LEAD-pinned hashes) and this session's precedent (lead-4c3z, lead-x8pw both CARRIED the lead-authored Gherkin+hash), the BC cannot mint the lead-held replacement pin itself, and cannot land the code fix green while the old 172 (leaf=bc-base) is the pinned scenario (its tests go RED). So this is a clarify, not a work_done.

ASK: adopt/pin (or correct) the CANDIDATE re-authored 172 below and re-issue lead-ss6k carrying it as an attached scenario (exactly as lead-x8pw carried its scenarios), so the BC can land the fix and the reviewer can echo a LEAD-pinned hash.

CANDIDATE re-authored 172 — recomputed block-only @scenario_hash (scenarios hash, ADR-019, Feature: line NOT in the hashed block) = 725562869d9df919 (supersedes da8ea23429b8bdc9). Changes from current 172: (a) leaf-image Then clause now pins literal "shopsystem-bc-lead" (launcher AND leaf both run bc-lead — the leaf needs the docker CLI to run bc-container launch itself); (b) ADDED a Then clause asserting the inner bc-container launch carries "--network" (product-scoped). Launcher-image assertion, forbidden-substring absences, and durable no-host-cred guarantees kept intact. Full Gherkin:

  @scenario_hash:725562869d9df919 @bc:shopsystem-templates
  Scenario Outline: bootstrap of a "lead" shop named "<slug>" writes "bin/shop-shell" as a thin wrapper that DELEGATES the brokered Claude launch to "bc-container" running in an ephemeral LAUNCHER image carrying the docker CLI ("shopsystem-bc-lead") — it brings up the compose supporting services, assembles the operator agent-vault "--env-file", then runs "bc-container launch" standing up the leaf-BC session ALSO on the "shopsystem-bc-lead" runtime image (the leaf needs the docker CLI too so its own router can run "bc-container launch"), attaching the leaf to the slug-scoped compose network via "--network" so it reaches postgres + agent-vault by compose hostname, mounting the lead repo as the workspace with the lead-only docker socket and a LEAD startup-prompt override, and "bc-container attach" — while constructing NO proxy URL, fetching NO CA, building NO shell image, and mounting NO host credentials
    Given an existing git repository at a target directory "/tmp/example-lead-shop" with no "bin/" subdirectory
    When I invoke the "shop-templates" bootstrap entry point with shop type "lead", shop name "<slug>", and target directory "/tmp/example-lead-shop"
    Then the exit code is 0
    And after the invocation the target directory contains a file at "bin/shop-shell" whose owner-execute permission bit is set and whose first line is exactly "#!/usr/bin/env bash"
    And the body of "bin/shop-shell" contains the literal substring "docker compose" followed somewhere later in the file by the literal substring "up -d postgres agent-vault", so it brings up both supporting services per ADR-028 D1
    And the body of "bin/shop-shell" assembles the operator agent-vault credentials into an env-file by referencing the literal substring "--env-file"
    And the body of "bin/shop-shell" launches the ephemeral LAUNCHER by the literal substring "docker run --rm" carrying the interactive flag literal substring "-it" and the launcher image reference by the literal substring "ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-lead:latest", because the launcher must carry the docker CLI to run "bc-container"
    And the body of "bin/shop-shell" mounts the host docker socket into that ephemeral launcher by the literal substring "/var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock"
    And the body of "bin/shop-shell" mounts the lead repository into that ephemeral launcher so the inner "bc-container" can see it
    And the body of "bin/shop-shell" inside that ephemeral launcher invokes "bc-container launch" carrying the literal substring "--workspace-mount" and the literal substring "--mount-docker-socket", so the launched lead session mounts the live lead working tree and is granted the lead-only docker-daemon access
    And the body of "bin/shop-shell" hands the leaf-BC session its runtime image by the literal substring "shopsystem-bc-lead", so the launched session runs on the lead-launcher image because the leaf needs the docker CLI to run "bc-container launch" itself
    And the body of "bin/shop-shell" attaches the launched leaf-BC session to the slug-scoped compose network by the inner "bc-container launch" carrying the literal substring "--network", so the launched session reaches the compose postgres and agent-vault by hostname (the outer launcher's network does not attach the separate leaf container)
    And the body of "bin/shop-shell" passes a lead-specific startup prompt into the launch by the literal substring "--startup-prompt", overriding the BC-default session-start so the launched session runs the router session-start
    And the body of "bin/shop-shell" drops the operator into the brokered session by the literal substring "bc-container attach"
    And the body of "bin/shop-shell" does not contain the literal substring "14322" and does not contain the literal substring "HTTPS_PROXY", because proxy-URL construction is delegated to the launcher and not re-derived in the wrapper
    And the body of "bin/shop-shell" does not contain the literal substring "agent-vault ca fetch" and does not contain the literal substring "agent-vault-check", because CA sourcing and the readiness check are delegated to the launcher and bc-base entrypoint
    And the body of "bin/shop-shell" does not contain the literal substring "SHOPSYSTEM_SHELL_IMAGE" and does not contain the literal substring "docker build", because the dedicated shell image and its Dockerfile are retired
    And the body of "bin/shop-shell" does not contain the literal substring "$HOME/.claude" and does not contain the literal substring "$HOME/.gitconfig" and does not contain the literal substring "~/.claude" and does not contain the literal substring "~/.gitconfig", so no host credentials are mounted

    Examples:
      | slug       |
      | shopsystem |
      | dummyco    |

notes:
Lead work_id: lead-mgwb
clarify: Carried scenarios 172 and 175 are mutually unsatisfiable on the dummyco render, so slice 2 cannot land green as pinned. Specifics: scenario 172 (@scenario_hash:03f1256aefc7fad4) is a Scenario Outline whose Examples table carries BOTH slugs (shopsystem AND dummyco); its step requires the rendered bin/shop-shell to contain the FIXED literal substring "shopsystem-bc-base" for EVERY example row, including dummyco. Scenario 175 (@scenario_hash:72dbfc6d64222885) renders shop name "dummyco" and requires the rendered bin/shop-shell to contain NO case-insensitive occurrence of the literal "shopsystem". For the dummyco render these directly contradict: the required substring "shopsystem-bc-base" itself contains "shopsystem", which 175 forbids. Both hashes recompute-match your pins via block-only `scenarios hash` (verified on disk), so this is not a transcription or recompute error — it is a contradiction in the carried ledger itself. The other 6 carried scenarios (134/174/139/140/141/137) are consistent and recompute-verified; the retire/supersede surgery is done and clean. Proposed resolution for you to confirm and re-pin: make 172's bc-base image reference SLUG-PARAMETRIC so the dummyco row renders "dummyco-bc-base" (and shopsystem renders "shopsystem-bc-base"), i.e. the step should pin the literal "<slug>-bc-base" / a "-bc-base" suffix rather than the fixed "shopsystem-bc-base"; OR drop the dummyco row from 172's Examples table; OR (if the bc-base image is intentionally a fixed product-neutral tag) re-pin BOTH 172 and 175 so the shared image literal is not a cross-product literal 175 forbids. Either way 172 (and possibly 175) needs a lead-authored replacement hash before I can land green. Holding slice 2 pending your re-pin.

notes:

clarify: PDR-020 slice2 is mostly actionable, but the ADR-018 BC-local reconcile (which the lead pre-state could not see) is wider than the four surgeries this dispatch enumerated (delete 183=2adc62a25c401e4b + 192=513cf8ea1a8ff4e9 files; remove old-172=5335c39eb06f7493 and 135=6f53ce53c10e3c09 blocks). Converging bin/shop-shell to the thin wrapper and retiring Dockerfile.shopsystem-shell ALSO contradicts these BC-local-ONLY pinned scenarios, none of which have a lead-provided replacement hash:

(A) Direct shop-shell-body conflict: 3d94639d5af360d7 (bootstrap_lead_ops_scaffolding.feature) pins that bin/shop-shell MUST reference SHOPSYSTEM_SHELL_IMAGE and 'docker run' against the shell image -- the new 5e42381f435397f2 forbids SHOPSYSTEM_SHELL_IMAGE outright. These cannot both hold.

(B) Dockerfile-existence / six-file-count conflicts (Dockerfile retired -> these go red): 8cf5656c55b466e7 (lead writes shop-owned Dockerfile.shopsystem-shell), cb1e585684ff4a14 ('exactly six' ops files incl Dockerfile.<slug>-shell), 1b6dbe8a0095fb8f (Dockerfile.dummyco-shell rendered, no-cross-product), d8b53704ce2e6584 ('.env.example additive to the six-file set' enumerating Dockerfile.<slug>-shell), and update-non-touch 3e8c8087c483db9e + ebbe3f1b92258299 + 59d41246cbd5235b (assert update leaves Dockerfile.shopsystem-shell untouched -- a file that will no longer exist). NOTE: 82c069bd3fb3b1d4 (BC shop writes NO Dockerfile) stays GREEN -- no conflict.

Several of these are multi-assertion scenarios mixing retired Dockerfile/SHELL_IMAGE claims with STILL-VALID coverage (compose, the agent-vault-provision/check pair, shop-scenario-completion, .env.example). I can RETIRE the pure-Dockerfile/SHELL_IMAGE ones, but the mixed ones (cb1e585684ff4a14 six-file->five-file, d8b53704ce2e6584, 3d94639d5af360d7, and the three update-coverage scenarios) need a REWRITTEN shape, which changes their @scenario_hash. ADR-010 forbids me from fabricating BC-local replacement pins.

REQUEST: please authorize the disposition for each, with lead-authored replacement @scenario_hash where a rewrite is needed:
 1. 3d94639d5af360d7 -> SUPERSEDE to a new hash (shop-shell with no SHELL_IMAGE / docker-run-shell-image), or is it fully covered by 5e42381f435397f2 + 03f1256aefc7fad4 and simply RETIRED?

 3. d8b53704ce2e6584 (.env.example 'additive to six-file set') -> SUPERSEDE to a five-file enumeration under a new hash?
 4. 8cf5656c55b466e7 + 1b6dbe8a0095fb8f -> RETIRE (Dockerfile gone), or SUPERSEDE the non-Dockerfile coverage of 1b6dbe8a0095fb8f (compose/shop-shell no-cross-product-literal) under a new hash?

title: RELEASE ENGINEERING (flat, no scenarios): cut the next PUBLISHED release of shopsystem-templates, strictly >0.18.0 (e.g. 0.19.0), from current origin/main HEAD. The cut commit MUST have ea21981 as an
BC sandbox lacks container-build tooling: Dockerfile dispatches cannot run mandated docker build + in-image PATH verification in-BC
description: Standing limitation surfaced by lead-30fs (empty-scenario request_bugfix fixing the shop-shell Dockerfile CLI-install step). The dispatch mandated an actual docker build of the rendered Dockerfile plus in-image 'which shop-msg/scenarios/shop-templates/claude' PATH checks. This BC container has NO container-build tooling: docker/podman/buildah/nerdctl all absent, no /var/run/docker.sock, no DOCKER_HOST. So any Dockerfile-touching dispatch can only achieve partial verification in-BC (render-test + isolated-venv install proof of the pinned specs + scenario test) and must hand the real docker build + in-image PATH check to a docker-capable host (the lead). Escaped risk / capability gap for future Dockerfile dispatches.

mechanism_observation: work-done-gate Check 1 cannot distinguish deliverable dirt from pre-existing harness/bd-export churn — work-done-gate Check 1 (clean working tree: 'any output from git status --porcelain -> fail, no exceptions, no partial passes') cannot distinguish DELIVERABLE dirt from pre-existing harness/registry-export churn, and as literally written would block a fully-integrated, deliverable-clean BC. On lead-y4pg the tree carried 3 dirty paths at emission, NONE of them this work's deliverable: .claude/settings.json and .claude/canonical/bc-primer.md (proven not this work's), and .beads/issues.jsonl (the bd Dolt-store JSONL export reflecting this session's tracking activity; authoritative state lives in .beads/embeddeddolt and passed Check 4). All deliverable dirs (features/ src/ tests/) were fully clean and origin/main-reachable. I signed off because the gate's PURPOSE (no uncommitted DELIVERABLE work) was met, but a literalist reviewer would have wrongly blocked complete and converted to blocked on unrelated churn. Secondary (lower-priority): Check 5 verifies only commit ORDER (test(red) before feat(green)), not that the red commit's tests actually FAILED at that commit. Per impl mechanism-obs #2, behavior 2's unit tests in red commit b2cba8d passed immediately against existing src (tautological); the genuine RED was the outer-loop scenario 3646efa06051fcac whose step defs were absent at b2cba8d and added in green b0b9199 (red/green diff is conftest-only).
